title: Biauricula resedifolia
taxonomy: Biauricula resedifolia is classified in the plant kingdom, specifically within the family Primulaceae. This plant is a member of the genus Biauricula, which is closely related to the genus Auricula. Biauricula resedifolia is a type of primrose that is native to Europe and is often used as an ornamental plant in gardens. The classification of Biauricula resedifolia is still being studied, but it is generally agreed upon that it belongs to the order Ericales. uses: Biauricula resedifolia, also known as Restharrow, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's roots and leaves contain compounds with potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Traditionally, Restharrow has been used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and indigestion, due to its purported ability to soothe the stomach and intestines.
